behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: This is algorithm-quality logic on in-memory inputs/outputs; no OS/network/subprocess or external side effect is required. MOCK-MISS is low because the behavior can be validated directly at the heuristic boundary by comparing heuristic vs computed optimal for bounded N. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text focusing only on route-length ratio for given inputs, with no external contract or infrastructure dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run parameterized/property tests on stop sets (size <= 20), compute optimal route length and heuristic length in-process, and assert heuristic/optimal <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily about module handoff (distance data + road-type speed config + estimator), so unit-only checks can miss wiring/config mapping faults (MOCK-MISS). Integration is sufficient because no real OS/network/subprocess is required, no SYSTEM TRIAD trigger is explicit, and no AI output is involved. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real external API contract validation or external round-trip side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the service layer with realistic route legs and road types using real config loading and distance-to-time computation modules; assert produced time windows match expected per-leg and cumulative timings.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic validator logic (comparison + arithmetic excess) at a single boundary with no required infrastructure or cross-module dependency in the behavior text. Rejection gate: Integration/System are disqualified because the behavior does not require proving persistence, network/subprocess behavior, or external readback; it only specifies rejection and excess computation.
behaviors.test_description: Call the validator with total route time and remaining shift hours across boundary cases (equal, slightly over, far over) and assert reject/accept status plus exact excess value.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Failure handling here is orchestration across modules (geocode result handling -> filtering -> optimizer -> skipped-address reporting), which unit-only tests can miss (MOCK-MISS). Integration with a deterministic geocoder stub that returns 'no result' for one address provides the needed reality signal. Rejection gate: System was considered but disqualified because the behavior text does not explicitly require validating the real provider HTTP response contract or other SYSTEM TRIAD round-trip proof.
behaviors.test_description: Run the route-optimization service end-to-end in-process with a test geocoder adapter that returns coordinates for most addresses and no result for one; assert skipped-address list contains the failed input and optimized route excludes it while remaining stops are routed.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans deduplication/aggregation and downstream route construction, so correctness depends on state handoff between preprocessing and optimizer modules (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency is required. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because no external contract or round-trip side effect is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the service with repeated addresses carrying distinct delivery items, then assert output contains one stop for that address with merged items and that route order includes that stop only once.
